_doc: Maps provider POI categories to gameplay roles + a name tier. Hybrid sources (ADR-016 amended by ADR-018): building GEOMETRY from OSM; commercial/venue/landmark POIs from OVERTURE (639 named, rich); street-furniture SMASHABLES from OSM (benches/bins/post boxes Overture lacks). Everything named-but-uncategorised defaults to named backdrop for recognisability, never stripped.

name_tiers.named_backdrop: Real name shown on the UNSMASHABLE backdrop/signage layer. Ambient, for orientation. Never destroyed. Free.
name_tiers.partner_interactive: The INTERACTIVE identity (branded beer anchor, named mission venue, AR check-in) is partner-only. Non-partner venues still work mechanically (generic beer / generic mission) but carry no brand in the interactive surface.
name_tiers.anonymous: No real name. Category flavour only.

roles.venue: Interactive candidate (beer anchor / mission / photo / future AR). Also shown as named backdrop. Carries an AR-candidate flag; real coords are NOT emitted into the shipped district until the venue becomes a partner (schema invariant).
roles.zone: Open area -> smashable-density zone (her buffet). No collider of its own.
roles.smashable: Real-placed street furniture. Dynamic body, small points, unnamed.
roles.backdrop: Named, unsmashable signage. The recognisability layer: the off-licence, the insurance office a corner is known by. No collider beyond its building, no interaction.
roles.ignore: Non-physical / abstract Overture parent tags with no premises.

backdrop_default._doc: Any Overture POI that is named and not matched above becomes named backdrop signage. This is the recognisability layer you asked for: banks, currency exchange, jewellers, barbers, pharmacies, estate agents, the off-licence, the insurance office. Real name on the unsmashable layer, near-zero perf cost (signage is texture, not geometry).

ignore_categories._doc: Abstract Overture parent tags / virtual businesses with no physical door. Only these are dropped; everything else with a name survives as backdrop.

curation_knobs._doc: 639 name labels would clutter the view. The curator caps visible labels to the N most salient (landmarks + venues first, then nearest), LOD-culls the rest. Perf is unaffected either way; this is legibility, not frame rate.
